description: Mean 2-meter air temperature from NASA POWER climatology dataset, averaged over the reference period 1981–2021.
limitation_note: Climatological average may not reflect recent El Niño/La Niña extremes. Point estimates interpolated from grid.

description: Annual total precipitation from CHIRPS (Climate Hazards Group InfraRed Precipitation with Station data), blending satellite and ground station data.
provider: Climate Hazards Center, UC Santa Barbara
limitation_note: Satellite-gauge blending introduces uncertainty in sparse gauge regions.

description: CGIAR-CSI Global Aridity Index (AI = mean annual precipitation / mean annual potential evapotranspiration). Values <0.05 = hyperarid, <0.2 = arid, <0.5 = semi-arid.
limitation_note: Based on 30-year climatology; does not capture inter-annual variability or recent trend shifts.

description: All-sky surface shortwave downwelling irradiance from NASA POWER, representing daily average solar energy available at the surface.
limitation_note: Grid-averaged values; local topography (hills, valleys) creates micro-variability not captured.

description: INFORM Risk Index composite hazard score combining flood, drought, cyclone, and heat wave exposure probabilities.
limitation_note: Aggregated to country or first-level admin boundary; does not capture local micro-risk variation.

description: Road network from OpenStreetMap contributors, including primary, secondary, and tertiary road classes. Coverage reflects volunteer mapping density.
limitation_note: Rural areas of sub-Saharan Africa may have incomplete road coverage. Verify with field teams.

description: Settlement locations and estimated population from WorldPop high-resolution population grids and UNHCR/OCHA settlement databases.
limitation_note: Population estimates have ±15% uncertainty in data-sparse areas. Nomadic populations systematically under-counted.

description: Percentage of households with grid electricity access from World Bank World Development Indicators, disaggregated to sub-national level via Global Electrification Database.
limitation_note: Sub-national disaggregation uses modeled estimates. Grid access ≠ reliable access (outages not captured).

description: Distance in kilometers to the nearest healthcare facility (clinic, health center, or hospital) based on WHO/KEMRI facility registers and travel time modeling.
limitation_note: Facility lists may omit informal health posts. Road quality affects travel time more than straight-line distance.

description: Proportion of population using improved drinking water sources (piped, borehole, protected well) from JMP/WHO-UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme.
limitation_note: 'Improved' source definition does not guarantee water quality. Functionality rates for rural infrastructure average 35–40% in East Africa.
